name: Qwen3-Coder-Next
description: Alibaba's coding MoE: 80B total parameters with only 3B active, a hybrid Gated DeltaNet + gated attention layout across 512 experts, and 262K context natively. Scores 70.6% on SWE-Bench Verified and 44.3% on SWE-Bench Pro — territory that recently demanded 10–20x the active compute.
why: The economics of agentic coding, moved. Apache-2.0 weights, a million Hugging Face downloads a month, and an activation footprint small enough that quantized builds run serious agent workloads on a single big GPU.
useCases: Self-hosted agentic coding behind vLLM or SGLang
Fine-tuning a coding specialist you actually own
audience: AI engineers, self-hosting developers, teams leaving API coding models

setup.requirements: 159GB of weights at BF16 — multi-GPU or a heavily quantized GGUF for single-card use. Served via vLLM, SGLang, or llama.cpp

license.notes: Apache-2.0 is declared by Qwen in the model-card metadata and README, but at check time neither the Hugging Face repo nor the QwenLM/Qwen3-Coder GitHub repo ships a standalone LICENSE file. Prior Qwen coder releases carried the full Apache text; we'd like to see the file return.

tally.lastActivity: Released Feb 2026; GGUF and ecosystem builds active

limitations: Full-precision inference is multi-GPU territory, the hardest agentic benchmarks still favor closed flagships, and the missing LICENSE file is a paperwork wobble worth watching.
take: Every few months the compute floor for real coding agents drops through another story. This is the current basement.
